result: If I had the power to change one thing, I’d cultivate a universal habit of genuine listening. If people regularly paused their own narratives long enough to truly absorb another’s perspective, we might find it easier to work through differences, craft policies rooted in shared humanity, and feel less alone. Empathy isn’t a cure-all, but everyday attention to each other could soften so many of our sharp edges.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
